commands.repair_reason: This is a protected maintainer-labeled PR with a security-boundary policy choice, so ClawSweeper should not open a repair branch unless maintainers first choose the desired token-scope behavior.; Needs attention: The patch intentionally expands a token scope that gates access to Talk secret material, so it needs explicit security-boundary approval before merge. commands.review_summary:
Adds `operator.talk.secrets` to the setup-code/bootstrap operator handoff allowlist and updates gateway/bootstrap pairing tests plus protocol and QR docs.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main’s bootstrap handoff allowlist excludes `operator.talk.secrets`, while `talk.config` rejects `includeSecrets` without that scope. I did not run a live gateway or mobile pairing flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: This is a protected maintainer-labeled PR with a security-boundary policy choice, so ClawSweeper should not open a repair branch unless maintainers first choose the desired token-scope behavior.

target.title: fix(gateway): include talk secrets in bootstrap handoff
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `operator.talk.secrets` to the bounded setup-code/bootstrap operator handoff scope list.
- Update bootstrap, device-pairing, setup-code, and control-UI expectations for the expanded handoff.
- Update protocol and QR docs so the documented trusted mobile handoff matches runtime behavior.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/device-bootstrap-profile.test.ts src/infra/device-pairing.test.ts src/gateway/server.auth.control-ui.suite.ts src/pairing/setup-code.test.ts src/infra/device-bootstrap.test.ts`
- `CODEX_REVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 bash /Users/guti/.codex/skills/codex-review/scripts/codex-review --mode branch --base origin/main`
- Remote source install on `guti@gutsy-home.local:~/projects/openclaw`: checked out `gateway-talk-secret-scope-main` at `dfd6fa3a8f`, ran `pnpm install`, rebuilt via `pnpm openclaw --version`, and restarted the LaunchAgent gateway.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: trusted setup-code/bootstrap handoff now carries `operator.talk.secrets` so native clients can read Talk config after bootstrap without granting admin or pairing scopes.

Real environment tested: local Codex worktree plus `guti@gutsy-home.local:~/projects/openclaw` source checkout running the managed Gateway LaunchAgent.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw gateway restart` on the remote source checkout, then `pnpm openclaw gateway status --deep`.

Evidence after fix: remote source CLI reported `OpenClaw 2026.5.22 (dfd6fa3)`; gateway status showed command `/opt/homebrew/opt/node/bin/node /Users/guti/projects/openclaw/dist/index.js gateway --port 18789`.

Observed result after fix: gateway runtime was active, connectivity probe was `ok`, and the running command pointed at the source checkout.

What was not tested: full release/packaging lanes and live mobile app pairing were not run; requester will perform manual app testing against the restarted source gateway.
target.author: ngutman
